REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND ON GROUNDS OF UNCERTAINTY AND FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS SHALL HAVE 30 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT CLEARLY LABELING WHICH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ASSERTED BY MR. CHAPMAN, MS. FOLASHADE, OR BOTH; AND WHICH DEFENDANT THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS ASSERTED AGAINST. IN ADDITION, THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE FAIR HOUSING ACT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. ON THE ONE HAND, PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANTS DENIED MR. CHAPMAN'S REQUEST FOR TRANSFER TO AN ACCESSIBLE UNIT BUT 16 ALLEGES DEFENDANTS DID OFFER PLAINTIFF CHAPMAN AN ACCESSIBLE UNIT BUT IT WAS REJECTED. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFFS' OWN FACTS DEMONSTRATE THAT IT IS SFHA THAT MUST APPROVE ANY REQUESTS FOR "BELOW MARKET RENT" FOR THE NEW UNIT, AND THEREFORE DEFENDANTS FILLMORE CENTER AND GREYSTAR DO NOT HAVE THE REQUISITE AUTHORITY TO GRANT THE RELIEF REQUESTED. THESE ALLEGATIONS MUST BE RECONCILED.(*PLS SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING*)PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Dec-19-2025 Continued To Mar-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ashley Smith for defendant San Francisco Housing Authority.
Somalia Folashade, plaintiff in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on Defendant's Motion for Order Dismissing Action Following Plaintiff's Failure to Amend.

Ms. Smith contested the Court's tentative decision to deny the motion. After hearing oral argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ORDER DISMISSING ACTION FOR FAILURE TO AMEND is continued to March 2, 2026. Plaintiffs to file and serve an amended complaint no later than February 18, 2026.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT GREYSTAR CALIFORNIA, INC., FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LLC DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend on grounds of uncertainty and for failure to state a cause of action. Plaintiffs shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint clearly labeling which causes of action are asserted by Mr. Chapman, Ms. Folashade, or both; and which defendant the cause of action is asserted against. In addition, the demurrer to the First Cause of Action for Violation of the Fair Housing Act is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. On the one hand, Plaintiffs allege that Defendants denied Mr. Chapman's request for transfer to an accessible unit but 16 alleges Defendants did offer Plaintiff Chapman an accessible unit but it was rejected. Moreover, Plaintiffs' own facts demonstrate that it is SFHA that must approve any requests for "below market rent" for the new unit, and therefore defendants Fillmore Center and Greystar do not have the requisite authority to grant the relief requested. These allegations must be reconciled.
The demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for Violation of the Americans with Disabilities Act is OVERRULED. The court in Independent Housing Services of San Francisco v. Fillmore Center Associates (1993) 840 F.Supp. 1328, cited by Defendants, states that the ADA does apply to the Fillmore Center. (Id. at 1344.)
The demurrer to the Third Cause of Action for Violations of the California Fair Employment and Housing Act is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for the same reasons as the First cause of action, i.e., there is nothing in the FAC supporting that Mr. Chapman requested an accommodation that he did not
receive.
The demurrer to the Fourth Cause of Action for Violations of the California Disabled persons Act is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for the same reasons as the First cause of action, i.e., there is nothing in the FAC supporting that Mr. Chapman requested an accommodation that he did not receive.
The demurrer to the Fifth Cause of Action for Violation of Civil Code section 51 is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for the same reasons as the First cause of action, i.e., there is nothing in the FAC supporting that Mr. Chapman requested an accommodation that he did not receive.
The demurrer to the Sixth Cause of Action for Breach of the Warranty of Habitability is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiffs to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action.
The demurrer to the Seventh Cause of Action for Constructive Eviction is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiffs to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action.
The demurrer to the Eighth Cause of Action for Negligence and Negligence per se is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiffs to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action.
The demurrer to the Ninth, Tenth, and Eleventh Causes of Action are OVERRULED to the extent that they were alleged against defendant SFHA, and not defendants Fillmore Center and Greystar.
The demurrer to the Twelfth Cause of Action for Fraud and Misrepresentation is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action with specificity; specifically, who made the misrepresentation and when and to whom.
The demurrer to the Thirteenth Cause of Action for IIED is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiff to allege facts regarding the contents of the discriminatory remarks and who they were made by and to whom.
